ontology.foodon_label: mixed grain and protein bowl food product
ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for a customisable chicken-egg grain bowl; FOODON_00002809 (mixed grain and protein bowl food product) used as closest defensible approximation.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite dish — grain-based bowl with poultry and egg
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (composite dish, grain-based with poultry) used as closest defensible code; no exact code exists for a four-topping customisable grain bowl with egg.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g, representing a complete grain bowl with chicken, egg, and four vegetable toppings as typically served at a hawker centre or health-food outlet in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken grain bowl with egg (4 toppings), no sauce — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database, record F08010009
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100 g nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F08010009 at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat not reported by HPB for this record; estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogue (cooked chicken breast + hard-boiled egg + mixed grains, ~1.0 g/100 g) and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — mixed grain and legume dishes, analogous composite grain bowl
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific HPB grain bowl variant. GI estimated at 48 (low) based on analogous mixed-grain dishes with protein and fibre from the University of Sydney GI Database (mixed grain rice dishes with protein, GI range 45–55). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (48 × 8.23 g × 3.5 serving factor) / 100 ≈ 9.0 per 350 g serving. The high protein content (9.65 g/100 g), dietary fibre (1.95 g/100 g), and absence of sauce further support a low GI classification.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium at 174 mg/100 g; no-sauce preparation eliminates the primary sodium source in comparable bowl dishes
hypertension.guidance: This no-sauce grain bowl is among the lowest-sodium variants in its category at 174 mg/100 g (~609 mg per 350 g serving), well within the recommended limit of 2000 mg/day; individuals with hypertension should maintain the no-sauce preparation and avoid adding soy sauce or high-sodium condiments at the table.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High protein (9.65 g/100 g), moderate fibre (1.95 g/100 g), and low sugar (1.94 g/100 g) collectively attenuate postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 48 and a glycemic load of approximately 9 per serving, this bowl is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; selecting a whole-grain base and avoiding sweet sauces or dressings post-serving will further optimise glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.0 g/100 g estimated), no deep-frying, lean protein sources, and fibre from grains and vegetable toppings support cardiovascular health
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The lean chicken and egg protein, mixed-grain base, and four vegetable toppings provide a heart-healthy macronutrient profile with low saturated fat and no added sauce; individuals with cardiovascular disease should continue to avoid high-sodium or high-fat condiments and choose unsaturated-fat-rich toppings such as avocado or edamame where available.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Four vegetable toppings maximise antioxidant and fibre intake; no sauce eliminates hidden pro-inflammatory additives
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This bowl is suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to its anti-inflammatory ingredient profile; if toppings include nightshade vegetables such as tomato or bell pepper, individuals should monitor personal symptom response, and adding omega-3-rich garnishes such as walnuts or flaxseed is encouraged.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken Grain Bowl with Egg (4 Toppings), No Sauce (HPB record F08010009) is a contemporary Singaporean health bowl consisting of cooked chicken and a whole egg served over a mixed-grain base — typically a blend of brown rice, quinoa, or multi-grain rice — accompanied by four freely chosen vegetable or protein toppings such as edamame, corn, broccoli, or roasted pumpkin, with no sauce added. The dish is assembled rather than cooked as a single unit, giving it a clean, lightly seasoned flavour profile with the natural sweetness of grains and the savoury richness of chicken and egg. The texture is varied: tender sliced or diced chicken, a firm or soft-boiled egg, chewy mixed grains, and crisp or roasted vegetable toppings. Nutritionally, this is one of the highest-protein variants in the grain bowl series at 9.65 g protein per 100 g, with a low sugar content of 1.94 g/100 g, moderate dietary fibre at 1.95 g/100 g, and a low sodium level of 174 mg/100 g owing to the absence of sauce. Energy density is modest at 101 kcal/100 g. The estimated glycemic index is 48 (low), making it appropriate for blood glucose management. It is halal-certified and suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The no-sauce preparation is the most sodium-favourable variant in this bowl series and is recommended for individuals on sodium-restricted diets.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo record F08010009 at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogue and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous mixed-grain dish and documented. All other fields constructed from approved sources or defensible analogues.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.0 g/100 g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central composite analogue (cooked chicken breast + hard-boiled egg + mixed grains); HPB record F08010009 does not report saturated fat directly. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific customisable grain bowl are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (mixed grain rice dishes with protein, GI range 45–55); no direct GI measurement exists for this HPB record. 4. Glycemic load per 350 g serving calculated as (48 × 8.23 × 3.5) / 100 ≈ 9.0. 5. Dietary tag 'low_calorie' from input preserved; at 101 kcal/100 g this is consistent with a low-energy-density classification. 6. This is a customisable dish; actual nutrient values will vary depending on the specific four toppings and grain blend selected.
